title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: City Sydney Parramatta assessed highest number applications Q1 2025, together accounting third total. Source: NSW Planning Portal. Last updated: March 2025.

title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: Digital contact grew steadily across four quarters, phone -person volumes declined. Q4, digital accounted two-thirds requests. Source: Service NSW. Last updated: June 2025.

title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: Incidents highest summer months, peaking December January. July recorded lowest monthly total year. Source: Transport NSW. Last updated: December 2024.

title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: App transactions grew fastest year, nearly doubling. Web remained largest channel. Assisted digital declined steadily self-service adoption increased. Source: Service NSW. Last updated: June 2025.

title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: Grant disbursements accelerated October, final quarter accounting half full-year total. Source: NSW Treasury. Last updated: December 2024.

title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: total number open cases grew steadily across priority levels. Critical cases grew fastest proportionally, nearly doubling January June. Source: Service NSW. Last updated: June 2025.

title: Charts and graphs
text: Summary: Applications higher complexity scores consistently took longer process. straightforward applications (score 30) resolved within 5 working days. Complex applications (score 70) averaged 18 days, significant variation. Source: NSW Planning Portal. Last updated: March 2025.

title: waratah
text: NSW Government typeface Public Sans. Recommendations: Install font computer. package attempt register embedded copy font can used even without installing font first. possible use graphics device supports modern features ragg::agg_png(). devices used default RStudio {ragg} installed. using HTML output (interactive plots tables), ensure document loads Public Sans Google Fonts (see ). Either add <head>: add CSS:

title: waratah
text: standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package - just 2 extra lines code:  Additional styling using waratah package - specify palette package:
code: library(waratah) library(palmerpenguins) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr)  long_text <- paste(   c(     "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do",     "eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim",     "ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut",     "aliquip ex ea commodo consequat."   ),   collapse = " " )  p1 <-   ggplot(penguins) +   geom_point(aes(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = flipper_length_mm,     colour = species,     size = body_mass_g   )) +   labs(     title = "Let's try some *italics* in the title",     subtitle = long_text,     dictionary = c(       bill_length_mm = "Bill length (mm)",       flipper_length_mm = "Flipper length (mm)",       species = "Species",       body_mass_g = "Body mass (g)"     ),     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   )  p1 #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p1 +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = pal_nsw()) +   theme_waratah() #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). p1 +   scale_colour_discrete(palette = pal_nsw(hue = "blues")) +   theme_waratah() #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: waratah
text: standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package - specifying palette:  Additional styling examples specifying font size, reversing colour palette:
code: p2 <-   filter_out(penguins, is.na(sex)) |>   ggplot() +   geom_point(     aes(       x = bill_length_mm,       y = bill_depth_mm,       fill = interaction(sex, species),     ),     shape = 21,     size = 3,     colour = "black",     alpha = 0.8   ) +   labs(     title = "Penguin bill dimensions",     subtitle = "Separated by *species* and *sex*",     dictionary = c(       bill_length_mm = "Bill length (mm)",       bill_depth_mm = "Bill depth (mm)"     ),     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   ) +   guides(     fill = legendry::guide_legend_group(       title = NULL,       ncol = 1,       direction = "horizontal",       override.aes = list(size = 3)     )   )  p2 + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Paired") p2 +   discrete_scale(     "fill",     palette = pal_paired(waratah:::nsw_palettes$default)   ) +   theme_waratah() p2 +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = pal_nsw(hue = "purples", variant = "aboriginal")) +   theme_waratah(base_text_size = 14) #> Warning: This manual palette can handle a maximum of 4 values. You have #> supplied 6

title: waratah
text: standard ggplot output  Styling using waratah package - specifying pallete, removing white background adjusting font size
code: p3 <-   filter_out(penguins, is.na(sex)) |>   ggplot(aes(x = species, fill = island)) +   geom_bar() +   labs(     dictionary = c(species = "Species", island = "Island"),     title = "Perfectly proportional penguins",     subtitle = "Where do they all live?",     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   ) +   facet_wrap(vars(sex))  p3 p3 +   scale_fill_discrete(palette = pal_nsw(hue = "purples")) +   theme_waratah(     background_colour = FALSE,     base_text_size = 14   ) +   guides(x = guide_axis(angle = 70)) +   theme(     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()   )

title: waratah
text: standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package - making background void, removing white background, font size:
code: p4 <-   ggplot(penguins) +   geom_bar(aes(1, fill = species)) +   geom_text(     aes(       1,       y = after_stat(count),       label = stage(         species,         after_stat = sprintf("%s\n%.0f%%", label, 100 * count / sum(count))       ),       colour = stage(         species,         after_scale = col_contrasting(colour)       )     ),     stat = "count",     position = position_stack(vjust = .5),     show.legend = FALSE   ) +   coord_radial(     theta = "y",     inner.radius = .5,     expand = FALSE,     rotate.angle = TRUE   ) +   labs(     title = "Does anyone know if penguins like donuts?",     subtitle = "Not sure, but we know there are three species in the dataset",     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   )  p4 p4 +   scale_fill_discrete(     aesthetics = c("fill", "colour"),     palette = pal_nsw(hue = "reds"),     guide = "none"   ) +   theme_waratah(     void = TRUE,     base_text_size = 14,     background_colour = FALSE   )

title: waratah
text: standard ggplot output:  Styling using waratah package - removing background, changing font size:
code: sentiment <- c(   "Strongly Disagree",   "Disagree",   "Neutral",   "Agree",   "Strongly Agree" )  survey_data <- tibble(   answer = factor(rep(sentiment, 2), levels = sentiment, ordered = TRUE),   percent = c(8, 12, 30, 40, 10, 6, 18, 24, 34, 18),   group = sort(rep(c("Male", "Female"), 5)) )  p5 <-   ggplot(     survey_data,     aes(x = percent, y = group, fill = answer)   ) +   geom_vline(aes(xintercept = 0, colour = from_theme(accent))) +   # note: likert is not a standard stat - it's defined and hidden for this example   geom_tile(stat = "likert", height = 0.8) +   labs(     title = "Let's find out!",     subtitle = "How much do they agree with the statement \"Doughnuts are delicious\"?",     caption = "Totally made up data!",     x = NULL,     y = NULL,     fill = "Answer"   ) +   theme(     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),     legend.position = "bottom"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) paste0(abs(x), "%"))  p5 + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "PiYG") p5 +   discrete_scale(     "fill",     palette = pal_stretch(waratah:::nsw_palettes$neg_to_pos),     breaks = sentiment   ) +   theme_waratah(     background_colour = FALSE,     base_text_size = 12   )

title: waratah
text: logo package PNG file - view use following code:
code: library(magick) #> Linking to ImageMagick 6.9.12.98 #> Enabled features: fontconfig, freetype, fftw, heic, lcms, pango, raw, webp, x11 #> Disabled features: cairo, ghostscript, rsvg #> Using 4 threads image_path <- system.file(   "images",   "nsw-gov-logo-primary.png",   package = "waratah" ) image_read(image_path)

what: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
title: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
text: goal waratah provide R tool kit styles, patterns standards following Digital NSW, NSW Design System

title: Easy data viz - NSW Government colour & typography design
text: can install development version waratah GitHub :

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
text: Depending structure data may wish combine palettes according pattern. helpers may come handy. pal_interleave() pal_paired() grouped data. pal_stretch() interpolates palette new discrete palette. col_contrasting() chooses colours based given background colours.

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
text: ... two vectors colours amount amount lighten colours paired set pal palette object bg, colours vector colours

title: Construct palette variants — pal_interleave
text: col_contrasting() vector colours length bg, pal_*() palette object.

what: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
title: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
text: Palettes created using NSW Design System. several named palettes can specified palette. use palettes based NSW Design System colour grid, either specify hue allow shade vary, specify shade allow hue vary. use Aboriginal colour grid, specify variant = "aboriginal".

title: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
code: nsw_colours  pal_nsw(   palette = waiver(),   hue = NA,   shade = NA,   variant = c("base", "aboriginal"),   direction = 1 )

title: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
text: palette Name palette: default, brand_default, neg_to_pos, colour_blind. hue Name index hue - see Details. Ignored palette specified. shade Name index shade - see Details. Ignored palette specified. variant Name palette variant. Ignored unless hue shade specified. direction Set -1 reverse order colours palette, 1 original order.

title: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
text: palette object (see palette constructors)

title: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
text: "base" variant supports: hue: greys, greens, teals, blues, purples, fucshias, reds, oranges, yellows, browns shade: dark, normal, light, lighter "aboriginal" variant supports: hue: reds, oranges, browns, yellows, greens, blues, purples, greys shade: dark, normal, light, lighter Anchor colours used create NSW colour palettes can also used stand-alone (e.g. nsw_colours$blue_01 returns hex code "#002664").

title: NSW Design System Colour Palettes — nsw_colours
code: library(scales)  pal_nsw() |> show_col()  pal_nsw(hue = "blues") |> show_col()  pal_nsw(hue = "reds", direction = -1) |> show_col()  pal_nsw(shade = "light") |> show_col()  pal_nsw(shade = "normal", variant = "aboriginal") |> show_col()   # you can interpolate colours by converting to a continuous scale pal_nsw(hue = "blues") |> as_continuous_pal() |> show_col()

title: reactable_theme() — reactable_theme
text: reactable_theme()

title: reactable_theme() — reactable_theme
code: reactable_theme(   colour = "blue_01",   title_font = "Public Sans",   base_font = "Public Sans",   base_text_size = 10,   textColor = NULL,   .colours = nsw_colours,   backgroundColor = TRUE,   borderColor = TRUE,   stripedColor = NULL,   ... )

title: reactable_theme() — reactable_theme
text: colour starting colour table. can ophelia_colours (e.g. "blue_01", "green_01", "purple_02"). title_font default title "Public Sans". change different font (e.g. match external publication requirement), simply change desired (e.g. "Times New Roman"). Make sure installed fonts want use device first! base_font default font also "Public Sans". want use different font (e.g. match external publication requirement), simply change desired (e.g. "Arial"). Make sure installed fonts want use device first! base_text_size Base text size. header size relative base text size. textColor can specify text colour like different borderColor stripedColor. .colours Used read nsw_colours. Please leave ! backgroundColor Logical. Can specified TRUE/FALSE. colour off_white #F2F2F2 borderColor colour horizontal lines rows. stripedColor fill colour alternate rows. ... parameters pass reactable::reactableTheme(). See ?reactable::reactableTheme documentation. examples can done reactable, see https://glin.github.io/reactable/articles/cookbook/cookbook.html.

title: reactable_theme() — reactable_theme
text: theme apply reactable object

title: reactable_theme() — reactable_theme
code: library(reactable) head(palmerpenguins::penguins, 10) |>   reactable(theme = reactableTheme())  {"x":{"tag":{"name":"Reactable","attribs":{"data":{"species":["Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie"],"island":["Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen"],"bill_length_mm":[39.1,39.5,40.3,"NA",36.7,39.3,38.9,39.2,34.1,42],"bill_depth_mm":[18.7,17.4,18,"NA",19.3,20.6,17.8,19.6,18.1,20.2],"flipper_length_mm":[181,186,195,"NA",193,190,181,195,193,190],"body_mass_g":[3750,3800,3250,"NA",3450,3650,3625,4675,3475,4250],"sex":["male","female","female",null,"female","male","female","male",null,null],"year":[2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007]},"columns":[{"id":"species","name":"species","type":"factor"},{"id":"island","name":"island","type":"factor"},{"id":"bill_length_mm","name":"bill_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"bill_depth_mm","name":"bill_depth_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"flipper_length_mm","name":"flipper_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"body_mass_g","name":"body_mass_g","type":"numeric"},{"id":"sex","name":"sex","type":"factor"},{"id":"year","name":"year","type":"numeric"}],"dataKey":"d09fc92c32ed54493f36a19991012b2e"},"children":[]},"class":"reactR_markup"},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]} # Adding NSW style: head(palmerpenguins::penguins, 10) |>   reactable(theme = reactable_theme())  {"x":{"tag":{"name":"Reactable","attribs":{"data":{"species":["Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie"],"island":["Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen"],"bill_length_mm":[39.1,39.5,40.3,"NA",36.7,39.3,38.9,39.2,34.1,42],"bill_depth_mm":[18.7,17.4,18,"NA",19.3,20.6,17.8,19.6,18.1,20.2],"flipper_length_mm":[181,186,195,"NA",193,190,181,195,193,190],"body_mass_g":[3750,3800,3250,"NA",3450,3650,3625,4675,3475,4250],"sex":["male","female","female",null,"female","male","female","male",null,null],"year":[2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007]},"columns":[{"id":"species","name":"species","type":"factor"},{"id":"island","name":"island","type":"factor"},{"id":"bill_length_mm","name":"bill_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"bill_depth_mm","name":"bill_depth_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"flipper_length_mm","name":"flipper_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"body_mass_g","name":"body_mass_g","type":"numeric"},{"id":"sex","name":"sex","type":"factor"},{"id":"year","name":"year","type":"numeric"}],"theme":{"borderColor":"#002664","stripedColor":"#0026644C","style":{"color":"#002664","fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"10px"},"tableStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"10px"},"headerStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"12px"},"cellStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"10px"}},"dataKey":"d09fc92c32ed54493f36a19991012b2e"},"children":[]},"class":"reactR_markup"},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]} # Further arguments for styling head(palmerpenguins::penguins, 10) |>   reactable(     theme = reactable_theme(       colour = "blue_01",       base_font = "Arial",       textColor = "black",       backgroundColor = TRUE,       borderColor = TRUE     ),     striped = TRUE   )  {"x":{"tag":{"name":"Reactable","attribs":{"data":{"species":["Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie","Adelie"],"island":["Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen","Torgersen"],"bill_length_mm":[39.1,39.5,40.3,"NA",36.7,39.3,38.9,39.2,34.1,42],"bill_depth_mm":[18.7,17.4,18,"NA",19.3,20.6,17.8,19.6,18.1,20.2],"flipper_length_mm":[181,186,195,"NA",193,190,181,195,193,190],"body_mass_g":[3750,3800,3250,"NA",3450,3650,3625,4675,3475,4250],"sex":["male","female","female",null,"female","male","female","male",null,null],"year":[2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007,2007]},"columns":[{"id":"species","name":"species","type":"factor"},{"id":"island","name":"island","type":"factor"},{"id":"bill_length_mm","name":"bill_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"bill_depth_mm","name":"bill_depth_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"flipper_length_mm","name":"flipper_length_mm","type":"numeric"},{"id":"body_mass_g","name":"body_mass_g","type":"numeric"},{"id":"sex","name":"sex","type":"factor"},{"id":"year","name":"year","type":"numeric"}],"striped":true,"theme":{"borderColor":"#002664","stripedColor":"#0026644C","style":{"color":"black","fontFamily":"Arial","fontSize":"10px"},"tableStyle":{"fontFamily":"Arial","fontSize":"10px"},"headerStyle":{"fontFamily":"Public Sans","fontSize":"12px"},"cellStyle":{"fontFamily":"Arial","fontSize":"10px"}},"dataKey":"d09fc92c32ed54493f36a19991012b2e"},"children":[]},"class":"reactR_markup"},"evals":[],"jsHooks":[]}

title: theme_waratah() — theme_waratah
text: theme_waratah()

title: theme_waratah() — theme_waratah
code: theme_waratah(   title_font = "Public Sans",   base_font = "Arial",   void = FALSE,   show_grid_lines = TRUE,   base_text_size = 11,   background_colour = TRUE,   .colours = nsw_colours )

title: theme_waratah() — theme_waratah
text: title_font default title \"Public Sans\". change different font (e.g. match external publication requirement), simply change desired (e.g. \"Arial\"). Make sure installed fonts want use device first! base_font default font \"Arial\"). want use different font (e.g. match external publication requirement), simply change desired (e.g. \"Times New Roman\"). Make sure installed fonts want use device first! void Logical (TRUE/FALSE). TRUE, grid lines axes removed. useful creating pie/donut charts. show_grid_lines Logical (TRUE/FALSE). FALSE, grid lines removed axis text retained. base_text_size Base text size pt. relative size title / subtitle text margins derived . background_colour Logical. default (TRUE) adds white colour background line ophelia colour palette. Change FALSE white background. .colours Used read waratah_colours. Please leave !

title: theme_waratah() — theme_waratah
text: object returned. Instead, theme applied ggplot plot.

title: theme_waratah() — theme_waratah
code: library(ggplot2)  p1 <-   ggplot(palmerpenguins::penguins) +   geom_point(aes(     x = bill_length_mm,     y = flipper_length_mm,     colour = species,     size = body_mass_g   )) +   labs(     dictionary = c(       bill_length_mm = "Bill length (mm)",       flipper_length_mm = "Flipper length (mm)",       species = "Species",       body_mass_g = "Body mass (g)"     ),     caption = "Data from {palmerpenguins}"   ) +   theme_waratah()

title: tooltipcss — tooltip_css
text: CSS code required add waratah-styled tooltips interactive graphs created using ggiraph

title: tooltipcss — tooltip_css
code: tooltip_css(   font_family = NULL,   font_size = NULL,   background_color = NULL,   text_color = NULL )

title: tooltipcss — tooltip_css
text: font_family Font family text tooltips (default: "Public Sans") font_size Font size text tooltips (default: "11px") background_color Hover background colour (default: #22272b) text_color Hover text colour (default: #F2F2F2)

title: tooltipcss — tooltip_css
text: character vector containing CSS styles tooltips

what: Tools for the New South Wales Design System in R — waratah-package
title: Tools for the New South Wales Design System in R — waratah-package
text: package allows NSW Government R users create data visualisations keeping NSW Design System. ensures consistent approach data visualisations appropriate colours fonts. package inspired data viz work Cara Thompson https://github.com/cararthompson.

title: Tools for the New South Wales Design System in R — waratah-package
text: Maintainer: Zoe Baldwin zoe.baldwin@health.nsw.gov.au (ORCID) contributors: Carl Suster carl.suster@sydney.edu.au (ORCID) [contributor]
